rulings.Case Title: RICHARD MUSGRAVE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment And For Leave To Defend Action
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 19, 2014 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Parker-Robb Chevrolet's motion to set aside default and default judgment and for leave to defend action is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ELAINE PERCY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Of Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 19, 2014 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to demonstrate that all of plaintiffs' claims against it fail even if defendant owed no duty to Elaine Percy. Duty is not an element of strict products liability. (Elsheref v. Applied Materials, Inc. (2014) 223 Cal.App.4th 451.) Defendant moved solely for summary judgment and did not include an alternate request for summary adjudication of individual causes of action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: TRUJILLO, MARY ANN, AS SUCCESSOR-IN-INTEREST TO et al VS. DURO DYNE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 19, 2014 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant FDCC California, Inc.'s motion to strike portions of plaintiffs' complaint is off calendar at the moving party's request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN MASERO et al VS. HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Appoint Michael Masero As Successor-In-Interest To, And Substitute Successor-In-Interest For, Deceased Plaintiff And For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 19, 2014 in Department 503, Line 4. Plaintiffs' and Petitioner's motion to appoint Michael Masero as successor-in-interest to, and substitute successor-in-interest for, deceased plaintiff John Masero and for leave to file first amended complaint is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
